title: Punjab’s Geography: The State That Feeds India
description:
Punjab may look small on the map, but its geography plays a huge role in feeding India. This video explains Punjab in the simplest way from the Shivalik Hills in the northeast to the fertile alluvial plains that make it the “Granary of India.”

We break down Punjab’s river system after partition, focusing on Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej, their origins, major dams, and how the Harike Barrage connects to the Indira Gandhi Canal. You’ll also learn how flooding brings fresh soil, why farming here is so intensive, and how limited forest cover has shaped the state’s environment.

Watch till the end and tell us which state’s geography you want next.

#Punjab #GeographyOfPunjab #IndianGeography #GranaryOfIndia #Sutlej #Beas #Ravi #IndianRivers #IndiaExplained #EducationalVideo #IndianStates #TheInformedCitizen
